title: artocarpus incisifolius var. apyrenus
intro: Artocarpus incisifolius var. apyrenus, also known as the Tamarillo-like Breadfruit or Philippine Breadfruit, is a variety of the Artocarpus incisifolius tree, native to the Philippines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, with its leaves and fruits possessing medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Artocarpus incisifolius var. apyrenus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, among others. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and fruits are often used to make teas, infusions, and poultices, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues.
